LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RANDY JORDON, GRAHAM COUNTY LAND COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GRAHAM COUNTY LAND COMPANY'S (GCL) DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jun-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRENT R. LAMAN, Esq. of Smith, Currie & Hancock LLP (Tel: 404-521-3800) for defendant and cross-complainant Graham County Land Company, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GRAHAM COUNTY LAND COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Graham County Land Company LLC's unopposed application to have Brent Laman appear as counsel pro hac vice is granted.

Counsel for the movant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar May-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS FLEXIBLE FUNDING LTD. LIABILITY CO., STEVE CAPPER, NASH LIAO, BILL HANSON and PAUL DELUCA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion to quash service of summons is granted. Summons was handed to a security guard at an office tower who was not employed by defendants. The guard was neither "in" defendants' office nor "apparently in charge thereof." (CCP 415.20.) Thus, the "thereafter mailing" was ineffective. (Id.) The opinions plaintiff cites are factually distinguishable. Further, defendants' counsel says he will accept service, so the dispute seems to be a tempest in a teapot.

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Michael I. Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP, 909.980.1040, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Daniel McLennon, Esq. of Currie & Hancock LLP, 415.394.6688, for the Defendants.

DEFENDANTS RANDY JORDON, GRAHAM COUNTY LAND COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Graham County Land Company's (GCL) demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is overruled in its entirety. The complaint sufficiently alleges the 8th and 9th causes of action for conversion and violation of Penal Code section 496(a).

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Michael I. Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP, 909.980.1040, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Daniel McLennon, Esq. of Currie & Hancock LLP, 415.394.6688, for the Defendants.

DEFENDANTS RANDY JORDON, GRAHAM COUNTY LAND COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter called. No argument is held on this motion. The tenative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Randy Jordan's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted.

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

The order will be reviewed by the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
